title: Cost per Equivalent Unit & Assignment (WA)
event: Continuing Reyes Co.'s Mixing Department: materials costs are $12,000 (beginning WIP) plus $88,000 added; conversion costs are $8,600 plus $57,400 added. Equivalent units are 50,000 for materials and 44,000 for conversion; ending WIP is 10,000 units (100% materials, 40% conversion). Compute cost per equivalent unit and the cost of ending WIP.

computationPaths.narration: Weighted average pools beginning-WIP costs with current costs, divides by EU, then assigns: completed units take full cost, ending WIP takes only the work done so far.

memorize.body: WA cost/EU = (beginning WIP cost + current cost) ÷ WA equivalent units — per cost category.
Ending WIP uses EQUIVALENT units (4,000 for conversion), never physical units. Assigned costs must sum back to total costs — always run the check.

questions.prompt: Using the rates above, cost of ending WIP = ?

distractors.feedback: You costed ending WIP as fully complete (10,000 × $3.50). Conversion is only 40% done: 20,000 + 6,000 = 26,000.
Materials only. Ending WIP also carries 4,000 equivalent units of conversion at $1.50.
